intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but represented only by the anchor (white rabbit) and a dark, indistinct mass in the soil.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: White rabbit anchor confirmed; plant matter is severely obscured/degraded.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine is the only clear feature; foliage is absent or collapsed into the substrate.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all containers; no visible fungal blooms or surface moss.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Debris-free, though lighting conditions are extremely low, hindering detection of micro-sprouts.
biome_observations.anomalies: The presence of a spoon in the yellow pot (P1) in the earliest image is an uncatalogued intervention.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Spoon removed from P1; white object (possibly a bottle or container) remains near P1/P3.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: Progressive loss of light and clarity; the scene has transitioned into a state of near-total visual obscurity, suggesting either a failure of the light source or a change in camera exposure settings.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total systemic collapse. There is no evidence of photosynthetic activity or viable plant tissue. The environment appears to be a dormant or failed botanical setup.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete failure of the botanical registry. The pots are essentially devoid of life, containing only soil and the white rabbit anchor. The chronological sequence shows a transition from a slightly more illuminated state to a near-blackout condition, rendering further biological assessment impossible without improved lighting.
